mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or top-quintile PAYEMS growth months do not produce significantly positive mean 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor demand that reduces household income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency falls below 45% when the trigger fires over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 60-day return in triggered months is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and retail sales indicates alignment between supply-side output and consumer demand, supporting corporate margin expansion and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the effect is absent when only one series is strong.
failure_condition: Top joint-quintile of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to outperform bottom joint-quintile by more than 2% annualized over any 3-year window, or interaction term Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.5%, an acceleration in consumer price inflation signals demand overheating that lifts nominal revenue growth faster than discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return hit rate in low-rate accelerating-inflation months is below 55% over any 2-year window, or mean 5-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are marked down.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency falls below 45% on INDPRO contraction signals over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day return in contraction months is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with a falling unemployment rate indicate dual strength in interest-sensitive consumption and labor income, amplifying expected earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or months with strong HOUST and falling UNRATE do not show positive mean 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales declining for two consecutive months triggers a consumer-spending retrenchment that drags down top-line revenue estimates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency is below 50% when the trigger fires over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 5-day return in triggered months is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized signals underlying momentum in aggregate demand; an upward growth surprise relative to trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reprice higher.
failure_condition: Positive return hit rate on GDP growth above 2.5% is below 55% over any 3-year window, or Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.2.
